##NAMES=Whiteite-(CaMnMg)
##RRUFFID=R140770
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CaMn^2+^Mg_2_Al_2_(PO_4_)_4_(OH)_2_·8H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Tip Top mine, Fourmile, Custer District, Custer County, South Dakota, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Thomas A. Loomis
##DESCRIPTION=Light tan with triangular striated faces, associated with deep red roscherite and colorless acicular sprays of tiptopite. Crystals are twinned by a 2-fold rotation around a*.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed only by single crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140770
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 14.709 b: 6.949 c: 9.929 alpha: 90 beta: 108.78 gamma: 90 volume: 960.8 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
